---
title: "Tall Threeawn (Aristida patula)"
slug: "aristida-patula"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180794302/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Tall Threeawn"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180794302/medium.jpg" alt="Tall Threeawn" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/betseyboughton">
betseyboughton</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107488925">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=158666)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare, Endemic species in Florida found in sandy fields, pine flatwoods, and prairie habitats.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its slender stems and distinctive three-branched awns extending from each seed. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as sandhills, scrub, and open pine woodlands. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to ground cover in fire-maintained ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/158666-Aristida-patula)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2441)
